discussion_comments.comment_body: All these are short titles?

Thank you for pointing that out — you're absolutely right!
The same short title was mistakenly used in all three tests.
I’ll correct them now by providing:
- A short title for the first test (as intended),
- A medium-length title to check font shrinking,
- A very long title to check line wrapping.

**issue**: Love the test, but I have a couple of improvements we can make to it:

- The test description doesn't make sense to me. Surely we should be using `getEffectiveHtmlLabels()` basically always? And this test doesn't actually check that `getEffectiveHtmlLabels()` is called, it's just testing what `getEffectiveHtmlLabels()` does.
- Should we move this into the `config.spec.ts` file? It seems to make more sense there.
- It would be even better to test this using `configApi.getConfig()` and `configApi.setSiteConfig()`, since that will handle default values properly. I think we're actually changing the behaviour right now.
resolved in e2084ff22a5b13fa4971fb1f4bfedb776a0be8ae

**suggestion**: Can we get rid of all these `saveConfigFromInitialize` calls you've added to the tests now that `getEffectiveHtmlLabels` doesn't actually use it?

It would make the tests much simpler to read.
resolved in 1249c14d8727c1dba437b4bf2941f19c9d044f27
Can these mocks be removed by using `jsdomIt`?
@sidharthv96 , cytoscape-cose-bilkent needs to stay mocked since JSDOM doesn’t support the plugin
That's fine, remove all mocks that can be removed.
resolved in 404fdaf2ff7936828828c387e8404e4e9cc625fc
See if you can use https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/develop/packages/mermaid/src/docs/community/contributing.md#testing-the-dom to avoid this.
resolved in 01e47333d5f0c8148b8d99acb9807184b8fa4319
Right now, the test only checks that some error is thrown. To make it more specific, please assert the actual error message so we can catch unintended error changes.
Noted, I'll do this.

Can't we use this approach?

```suggestion
  jsdomIt('appends the title as a text item with the given title text', function ({ svg }) {
```
We can, I missed it in the refactoring.
Updated in https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/pull/6161/commits/6836e9c7a89d125aea2d230a3f7e58889358f528

I'm guessing you're going to do state diagrams next?

Maybe it's worth putting this in a separate test, marking it with `it.fails` and have a comment that says something like: `// Sequence Diagram currently uses a singleton DB, so this test will fail`.
Added in https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/pull/6161/commits/6836e9c7a89d125aea2d230a3f7e58889358f528
